scope: full checkout (~2,291 files; 1,006 source files indexed → 22,116 chunks)

difficulty_legend.easy: direct symbol/keyword lookup; a named function/struct exists
difficulty_legend.medium: concept located within one subsystem; some synthesis
difficulty_legend.hard: cross-cutting / conceptual; no single obvious keyword

questions.q: How does a SQL string become an executable bytecode program end to end?
How does the optimizer decide to use a covering index to avoid a table lookup?
How are OR conditions turned into a multi-index (index-union) scan?
How does a materialized/incremental view propagate a single base-table change to its output?
How does an approximate vector index get built and queried, tying vector distance to the index method?
How is the database schema loaded from sqlite_schema and made available to the planner?
How does cell overflow (payload larger than a page) get written and read back?
How does the pager evict pages under memory pressure without losing dirty data?
How is the async IO completion model abstracted across platforms (io_uring / IOCP / generic)?
How does MVCC garbage-collect old row versions that are no longer visible to any reader?
How does the VDBE resume a partially-executed instruction across IO suspension points?
How does the sync engine reconcile local and remote WAL/frames?
How does the serverless layer expose the database over the network?
How is a correlated subquery in a WHERE clause compiled when it cannot be flattened?
How does constant folding / expression simplification happen before code generation?
How does printf-style formatting handle width, precision and type specifiers safely?
How is JSONB binary format decoded back into typed DB values?
How does the freelist track and reclaim trunk/leaf pages during deletes?
How are checkpoint locks acquired and released to coordinate readers and the checkpointer?
How are encrypted WAL/page chunks read and decrypted on the fly?
How does the engine implement a grace hash join that spills partitions to disk?
How does the planner handle triggers firing as part of a DML statement's execution?
How does the engine perform exact decimal arithmetic to avoid float rounding errors?
How is a snapshot of the WAL validated/rebuilt when an authority change is detected?
